name: app-service
context: Deploy and update Azure App Service web apps with `az webapp up`, zip artifacts, settings, and live logs
info: Azure App Service is the managed web-hosting surface for long-running HTTP applications. Agents use this skill to create or update a web app directly from a working directory, deploy a prepared zip artifact to an existing site, set application settings, attach identities in adjacent workflows, and tail live logs during rollout verification. The key CLI surface is `az webapp`.
triggers: deploy an app service to azure
zip deploy a web app in azure
set app settings on azure web app
publish a python app to azure
deploy a node app to azure app service
body:
# Azure App Service

`az webapp up` is the fastest path when the agent is holding a working directory and wants Azure to create missing resources and deploy the app in one pass. `az webapp deploy` is the better fit when the app already exists and the deployable artifact is a built zip file.

~~~bash
# From the project directory
az webapp up -n dojo-web-prod -g rg-dojo-web -l eastus --runtime "PYTHON:3.12"

# Deploy a built zip artifact to an existing app
az webapp deploy --resource-group rg-dojo-web --name dojo-web-prod --src-path ./dist/app.zip --type zip

# Update runtime configuration or secrets as app settings
az webapp config appsettings set \
  --resource-group rg-dojo-web \
  --name dojo-web-prod \
  --settings API_BASE_URL=https://api.example.com FEATURE_FLAG=true

# Watch live logs during validation
az webapp log tail --resource-group rg-dojo-web --name dojo-web-prod
~~~

Microsoft Learn notes that `az webapp up` creates a default resource group and App Service plan if needed, zip deploys the current directory with build automation enabled, and caches parameters in `.azure/config` for later runs. That makes it fast, but it also means an agent should inspect the cached config when the local folder may have been reused for a different environment.

Use [[azure/key-vault]] for secret storage instead of hard-coding application settings, and use [[azure/monitor]] immediately after deploy to verify resource health and control-plane events.

sections.title: Fast path with webapp up
sections.body: `az webapp up` creates or reuses the supporting plan and web app, deploys from the current directory, and stores local defaults in `.azure/config`. Use it when the source tree is present and a one-command deployment flow is more valuable than fine-grained control.

sections.title: Zip deploy existing apps
sections.body: `az webapp deploy --src-path ... --type zip` is the more explicit path once the site already exists and the build artifact is prepared elsewhere. This is the better fit for CI systems that separate build from deploy and want deterministic artifacts.

sections.title: Settings and log streaming
sections.body: `az webapp config appsettings set` pushes key-value settings, while `az webapp log tail` provides live log tracing during rollout verification. Use app settings for runtime configuration, but keep high-value secrets in [[azure/key-vault]] and inject them through managed identity or app configuration patterns when possible.

links.context: Authenticate and select the right subscription before deployment
Create or verify the target group for the web app
Store secrets outside the app settings payload when possible
Inspect metrics and activity logs immediately after deployment
Build and publish zip artifacts from CI before deploying them here

related.note: Key Vault is the right place for secrets that should not live in static settings
Vercel is an alternative managed web deployment surface for frontend-heavy apps
Cloudflare Pages is another hosted deployment target for web applications

scripts.name: Deploy Web App
scripts.description: Create or update an Azure App Service web app, set app settings, or stream logs

AZURE_APP_SERVICE_ACTION.description: One of up, deploy-zip, settings-set, or log-tail

AZURE_WEBAPP_RUNTIME.description: Runtime string for `az webapp up`, for example PYTHON:3.12

AZURE_WEBAPP_SKU.description: App Service plan SKU when using `webapp up`

AZURE_SUBSCRIPTION_ID.description: Subscription ID or name to activate before the command

depends.reason: Deployment and configuration changes require a valid Azure login
Web apps deploy into a chosen resource group
The execution wrapper is implemented in Bash
